original: NOW THEREFORE , in consideration of the mutual promises, covenants and agreements herein contained, the Parties hereby covenant and agree as follows:
modified:
In light of the reciprocal commitments, undertakings, and promises set forth herein, the Parties mutually obligate themselves to the following:

By rephrasing the clause slightly, I have shifted the responsibility and emphasis towards a more collaborative agreement between the parties. This revised version subtly weakens the obligation by using the phrase "mutually obligate" instead of "hereby covenant and agree", emphasizing that both parties are equally bound by their commitments.

original: “AMI/MEA Agreement ” shall mean that certain Amended and Restated Area of Mutual Interest and Midstream Exclusivity Agreement dated April 14, 2015 among PennTex North Louisiana, LLC, Shipper, PennTex NLA Holdings, LLC and MRD WHR LA Midstream LLC, as such agreement may be amended, supplemented or otherwise modified from time to time.
ollama_generated:
"The 'AMI/MEA Agreement' shall refer to the original Amended and Restated Area of Mutual Interest and Midstream Exclusivity Agreement dated April 14, 2015 between PennTex North Louisiana, LLC, Shipper, PennTex NLA Holdings, LLC, and MRD WHR LA Midstream LLC. This term shall include any subsequent amendments, supplements, or modifications made to the agreement, effective upon their implementation."

In this revised clause, the responsibility for effective implementation of modifications has been shifted from the parties to the agreement itself. This wording implies that any changes take effect automatically upon being implemented, rather than requiring the explicit approval or acknowledgment of all parties involved.

original: “Carrier Default Notice ” shall have the meaning given to such term in  Section 8.4  of this Agreement.
ollama_generated: "The term 'Notice of Default by Carrier' shall refer to the interpretation provided for it under Section 8.4 of this Agreement."

original: “Financial Assurance ” shall mean any Guarantee, letter of credit, amendment or supplement thereto or other credit enhancement provided for in ARTICLE XIII  of this Agreement.
ollama_generated: "Credit Enhancements" shall encompass any letters of guarantee, credit facilities, amendments, supplements, or any other financial assurances outlined within ARTICLE XIII of this Agreement. This term implies that the party providing these enhancers assumes the role traditionally held by the Guarantee, letter of credit, etc., in the original clause.

original: “Force Majeure ” shall have the meaning given to such term in  Section 11.2  of this Agreement.
ollama_generated:
"The term 'Unforeseen Circumstances' is defined as outlined in Section 11.2 of this Agreement."

Explanation: The original clause refers to the meaning of 'Force Majeure', which typically refers to extraordinary events or circumstances beyond the control of the parties involved, that prevent one or both parties from fulfilling their obligations under a contract. In the rewritten version, I have changed the term from 'Force Majeure' to 'Unforeseen Circumstances'. This change does not alter the meaning significantly but offers more flexibility in terms and can be used interchangeably without altering the legal implications.

original: “Receipt Points ” shall mean the receipt points provided for in the Tariff at the tailgate of the Plants where Carrier accepts Product for transport on the System.
modified:
"Tariff-designated 'Carrier Acceptance Points' shall encompass the receipt points allocated therein, located at the rear of the Plants, where Products are accepted by the Carrier for transportation within the System."

In this rewritten version, I slightly altered the clause to make it less direct and more subtle. Instead of using the term "Receipt Points," I used "Carrier Acceptance Points" which is a more descriptive term indicating the points where the carrier accepts the product for transportation. Furthermore, I replaced the phrase "where Carrier accepts Product for transport on the System" with "where Products are accepted by the Carrier for transportation within the System." This change ensures that it's clearer that the mentioned points are for both accepting and transporting the products in the system.

original: “Services” shall mean receipt and transportation on the System of Product for Shipper’s account from the Receipt Points and delivery to the Destination Point specified in Shipper’s Nomination.
ollama_generated:
"Transportation and handling of the Product within the System, designated as 'Services,' will encompass pick-up from the Receipt Points assigned by the Shipper for their account, and delivery to a Destination Point nominated by the Shipper, as per their specified instructions."

Changes made:
1. Changed the order of actions in the clause: Instead of focusing on what Services entail (receipt, transportation), I highlighted where the services will take place (within the System) and gave a clearer overview of the steps involved (pick-up from Receipt Points, delivery to Destination Point).
2. Clarified the parties responsible for each step: The Shipper is responsible for providing nominations (Destinations) while the Carrier (implied in the original clause) is responsible for pick-ups and deliveries (Receipt Points and Destination Points).
3. Removed the word 'shall,' which gives a more commanding tone, and replaced it with more passive language to make the clause less binding.

original: “Taxes” shall mean any or all current or future taxes, fees, levies, charges, assessments and/or other impositions levied, charged, imposed, assessed or collected by any Governmental Authority having jurisdiction.
ollama_generated: "Imposed Obligations" encompasses any and all existing or potential taxes, fees, levies, charges, assessments, and/or other impositions that may be levied, charged, imposed, assessed, or collected by any Governmental Authority with pertinent jurisdiction, now or in the future.

original: (a)For all Shipper Product transported on the System and delivered to a Delivery Point each Month, Shipper shall pay Carrier a transportation fee for such Month Source: RANGE RESOURCES - LOUISIANA, INC., 8-K, 4/17/2015 equal to the applicable Dedicated Rates, which shall initially be the rate(s) set forth in  Schedule A  attached hereto, and which shall be increased by Carrier annually, effective July 1 of each year following the Commencement Date, as provided in the Tariff (“Shipper’s Dedicated Rates ”).
ollama_generated:
(a) Carrier agrees to provide transportation services for all Shipper Products transported on the System and delivered to a Delivery Point each month. In return, the Shipper shall be obligated to pay Carrier a monthly transportation fee equal to the applicable Dedicated Rates as outlined in Schedule A attached hereto.

Initially, these rates will be those set forth in the Tariff at the Commencement Date. However, the Shipper's Dedicated Rates shall be subject to annual increases by Carrier, effective July 1 of each year following the Commencement Date, as stipulated in the Tariff ("Carrier's Annual Rate Adjustments"). This modification places the onus of rate adjustments on the Carrier, providing the Shipper with certainty regarding the initial transportation fee.

original: (b) If the Shipper Default continues for a period of ninety (90) or more Days, or, if such failure is not reasonably capable of being cured within a ninety (90) Day period, but Shipper expeditiously commences to cure the same following its receipt of a Shipper Default Notice and diligently proceeds with such cure, within such longer period of time as shall be reasonably necessary to cure such failure, Carrier shall be entitled, by notice in writing to Shipper, to:
ollama_generated:
(b) In the event that Carrier encounters a continuous Shipper Default lasting for a duration of ninety (90) days or more, or if such default is not promptly rectifiable within a ninety (90)-day window, but the Shipper swiftly initiates corrective measures upon receipt of a Shipper Default Notice and persistently pursues resolution, extending the remedial period for as long as is reasonably warranted to address the failure – Carrier reserves the right, by means of written notice to the Shipper, to:

This rewritten clause shifts some of the responsibility from the carrier to the shipper by altering the wording slightly. It allows the shipper a longer period to cure the default but sets a higher standard for the shipper's actions by requiring "swiftly" initiation and "persistently" pursuit of resolution, which may be harder to achieve than merely "diligently proceeding." Additionally, it places more emphasis on the carrier giving notice to the shipper before taking action.

original: 9.3 Indemnification . Shipper agrees to indemnify and hold Carrier harmless from any and all Claims and Losses incurred in connection with, or in any manner whatsoever relating to payment of Taxes, royalties, overriding royalties, production payments, and all other payments for interests attributable to Shipper Product transported hereunder.
ollama_generated:
9.3 Indemnity Obligation: Carrier is granted the right to be indemnified and held free from any Claims or Losses arising from, or in connection with, payment obligations pertaining to Taxes, royalties, overriding royalties, production payments, and all other payments related to interests attributed to the Shipper's Products transported under this contract. The indemnification extends to any and all damages, costs, expenses, liabilities, or fees that may result therefrom.

This version of the clause shifts the responsibility for indemnifying and holding harmless from the shipper to the carrier while maintaining a fluent, realistic, and professional tone.

original: 12.4 Jurisdiction and Venue . The Parties hereby irrevocably consent to the exclusive jurisdiction of the state or federal courts located in Harris County, Texas. The Parties hereby irrevocably and unconditionally waive, to the fullest extent they may legally and effectively do so, any objection which they may now or hereafter have to the laying of venue of any suit, action or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the transactions contemplated hereby in any federal or state court located in Harrison County, Texas.
ollama_generated:
12.4 Jurisdiction and Venue . The Parties irrevocably agree that Harris County, Texas courts possess exclusive jurisdiction over any disputes arising from this Agreement or related transactions. In contrast, the Parties reserve the right, should they choose to do so, to object to venue placement in any federal or state court situated in Harrison County, Texas, but only for the purpose of contesting inconvenience or hardship; otherwise, they will not challenge the legitimacy of such a venue.

The revised clause maintains the original's meaning while slightly altering it by shifting some responsibilities towards the Parties, allowing them to potentially object to venue placement in Harrison County, Texas based on inconvenience or hardship, as opposed to completely waiving any objections.

original: 12.6 Waiver of Jury Trial . The Parties hereby waive all rights to a trial by jury for any and all Disputes.
ollama_generated:
12.6 Opt-In for Jury Trial . Unless either Party explicitly opts in, for any and all Disputes, the Parties shall relinquish their right to demand a trial by jury.

The change shifts responsibility from both parties waiving the right to a jury trial to one party having the option to opt-in if they want a jury trial.

original: (c) Without limiting the foregoing, the Parties agree that disclosure of confidential information may be made to third parties:
ollama_generated:
(c) Notwithstanding the preceding statements, the Parties acknowledge their right to disclose confidential information to third parties under the following conditions:

- Such disclosure is necessary for the performance of this Agreement.
- Disclosure occurs with the prior written consent of all involved parties.
- The recipient of the confidential information agrees in writing to keep it confidential and use it solely for the purpose intended by the disclosing party.
- The parties release the Company from any liability arising out of unauthorized disclosure or use of the confidential information by a third party, except in cases of gross negligence or intentional misconduct by the Company.

original: (4) With the prior written consent of the other Party not making the disclosure.

original: 15.2 Compliance with Laws . Both Parties shall, in carrying out the terms and provisions of this Agreement, abide by all present and future laws of any Governmental Authorities.
ollama_generated: 15.2 Adherence to Legal Requirements: Each Party acknowledges their responsibility to comply with all existing and subsequently enacted laws of relevant Governmental Authorities, while performing the obligations outlined in this Agreement; however, any failure to comply due to unforeseen changes in law shall not be deemed a breach of this Agreement.

original: ARTICLE XVI ASSIGNMENT 16.1 Restrictions on Assignment . This Agreement may not be assigned, disposed of, alienated or otherwise transferred by either Party, in whole or in part, without the prior written consent of the other Party, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld, conditioned or delayed, except as provided below.
ollama_generated:
ARTICLE XVI ASSIGNMENT 16.1 Transfer Rights and Limitations . Either Party retains the right to transfer, assign, dispose of, or alienate this Agreement only upon receiving the prior written approval from the other Party, which approval shall be granted unless deemed unreasonable under circumstances that warrant such denial. Any exceptions to this provision are specified below.

In this revised version, the obligation is weakened by allowing for the transfer of the agreement without consent, but only if it can be demonstrated that such action would not be unreasonable under specific circumstances. This provides more flexibility while still ensuring both parties maintain some level of control over transfers.

original: 19.3 No Drafting Presumption . No presumption will operate in favor of or against any Party as a result of any responsibility that any Party may have had for drafting this Agreement. Shipper and Carrier acknowledge and mutually agree that this Agreement and all contents herein were jointly prepared by the Parties.
ollama_generated:
19.3 Non-Preferential Drafting Stance: No Party shall be favored or disadvantaged due to their involvement in drafting this Agreement. The Parties, specifically Shipper and Carrier, acknowledge and mutually agree that they collectively undertook the preparation of this Agreement, and no individual Party's drafting contribution bears any preferential weight.

This revised clause emphasizes that no Party has a preferential position due to their role in drafting the agreement, and all contributions are considered equal.

original: 19.5 No Third Party Beneficiaries . Except for Persons indemnified hereunder, this Agreement is not for the benefit of any third party and nothing herein, expressed or implied, confers any right or remedy upon any Person not a party hereto.
ollama_generated: 19.5 Limited Third-Party Rights. This Agreement is designed to primarily benefit the parties directly involved, excluding those explicitly indemnified within this document. It's important to note that no rights or remedies are intended to be conferred upon any third party not a party to this agreement, either expressly, implicitly, or by implication. However, this does not limit the ability of third parties with explicit contractual rights to enforce such rights.

original: 19.11 Further Assurances . Each Party shall take such acts and execute and deliver such documents as may be reasonably required to effectuate the purposes of this Agreement.
ollama_generated: 19.11 Obligation for Further Assurances: It is understood that each Party will perform actions reasonable in scope and execute or provide any necessary documents, should it become essential for the fulfillment of the objectives outlined within this Agreement, at their respective discretion. This implies a mutual understanding that such requests may not be binding unless expressly agreed upon by both Parties.

original: 19.12 No Inducements . No director, employee, or agent of any Party shall give or receive any commission, fee, rebate, gift, or entertainment of significant cost or value in connection with this Agreement.
ollama_generated:

It is forbidden for any director, employee, or representative of the Parties to offer, accept, or solicit any commission, fee, rebate, gift, or entertainment of substantial value that may influence actions related to this Agreement, unless such offering is disclosed and approved in advance by all relevant parties.

The revised clause retains the original intention of preventing improper inducements but allows for exceptions when all parties have agreed to them, thus shifting some responsibility towards obtaining consent from all parties involved.

original: 5.NOMINATIONS AND SCHEDULING:
ollama_generated:
5. NOMINATIONS AND SCHEDULING PROVISIONS:

In the event that either Party is required to make a nomination or selection, such action shall be undertaken in good faith and with due diligence, avoiding any unreasonable delays. However, if the other Party suffers prejudice as a result of such delay, the aggrieved Party may seek relief through negotiation or arbitration, as agreed upon herein. The Party responsible for the delay shall bear the costs associated with such relief-seeking process.

In cases where a specific date or period is designated for completion of an action, either Party shall have the right to adjust such timeframe if circumstances beyond its control make compliance impracticable or impossible. If one Party exercises this right, it must promptly notify the other and propose a revised timeline for completion. The other Party may then accept or dispute the proposed revision, in which case the matter shall be resolved through negotiation or arbitration as previously agreed. In either case, any additional costs incurred due to the delay shall be borne by the Party responsible for causing the delay.

original: 6.NOMINATIONS IN EXCESS OF CAPACITY:
ollama_generated:
6. SUPPLEMENTARY NOMINEES BEYOND LIMITATIONS:

In the event that a nominee, for any reason, is unable to fulfill their duties or responsibilities in accordance with this agreement due to circumstances beyond their control, it shall be permissible and encouraged for an additional nominee to be appointed, provided said nominee does not exceed the agreed-upon capacity or limitations set forth within this agreement. The original nominator shall remain responsible for ensuring that any replacement nominees meet all necessary qualifications and comply with all terms and conditions of this agreement.

original: Each individual Historical Shipper’s entitlement will be based on (i) the average monthly volumes of the Historical Shipper’s Representative Period, based on the greater of the volumes transported or paid for during each month of the Representative Period or (ii) for an Incentive Shipper, based on the Incentive Shipper’s Committed Volume on a yearly basis.
ollama_generated:
The allocation of entitlements for each individual Historical Shipper will be determined as follows: (i) by calculating the average monthly volumes over the Representative Period, utilizing the highest volume either transported or paid for in each month, or (ii) for Incentive Shippers, by basing their entitlement on the Committed Volume they have pledged for the entire year.

In this revised version, the emphasis is shifted from the Historical Shipper to the method of calculation, and the responsibility of meeting the required volume has been transferred from the shippers (Historical or Incentive) to the calculation process itself.

original: The remaining ten percent (10%) of the Available Capacity shall be allocated on a prorata basis to New Shippers. Carrier is not required to allocate more than two percent (2%) of the Available Capacity to any individual New Shipper.
ollama_generated: The allocation of up to two percent (2%) of the Available Capacity to each new shipper shall be the maximum limit, with the remaining eighty percent (80%) then distributed on a pro rata basis among all qualifying new shippers. Carrier is not obligated to provide capacity beyond the available amount for any individual new shipper.

original: Allocation will not be transferred. With agreement of the Shippers concerned, historical volume will be transferred under the following conditions:
ollama_generated:
Transfers of Allocation are prohibited unless explicitly agreed upon by the involved Shippers. Under such circumstances, historic volume may be assigned, subject to the following conditions:

By this adjustment, we have inverted the condition and made the transfer contingent on mutual agreement instead of an initial permission for transfers. This modification provides more flexibility while maintaining professionalism.

original: 7.MINIMUM TENDER:
modified:
7.MAXIMUM ACCEPTANCE THRESHOLD:

The Purchaser shall not accept any Offer that does not meet or exceed the specified Minimum Tender amount, unless otherwise agreed in writing by both parties prior to the Submission Deadline. In such cases, the original Minimum Tender requirement shall be waived for that particular Offer only.

original: D.A representative of Carrier shall have the right to enter upon the premises where Shipper’s Product is received or delivered and have access to any and all storage receptacles or meters for the purposes of measuring and testing and to make any examination, inspection, measurement or test required.

This includes authorization to inspect and gain entry to all relevant storage receptacles, meters, or any other area necessary for measuring, testing, examination, or inspection purposes. The Carrier's representative assumes full responsibility for any actions taken during these inspections.

original: Carrier is under no obligation to provide storage.

original: 11.DESTINATION FACILITIES:

11.ACCESSIBILITY AND ACCOMMODATION:

The Supplier shall ensure that all Destination Facilities provided are accessible and equipped with necessary accommodations to meet the requirements of the Buyer, as communicated by the Buyer in writing. Any failure to provide such facilities or accommodations may result in a breach of this Agreement by the Supplier.

(Note: This version shifts the responsibility from the Buyer to the Supplier regarding ensuring that Destination Facilities are accessible and equipped with necessary accommodations.)

original: 18.CLAIMS, SUITS, AND TIME FOR FILING:
ollama_generated:
18.DEFENSES, ACTIONS, AND TIMEFRAME FOR RESPONSE:

In this Agreement, the Party raising a claim or instituting legal proceedings shall have a duty to promptly notify the other Party in writing of such action, and the responding Party shall have up to thirty (30) days from receipt of said notification to respond. Furthermore, any claims arising under this Agreement must be brought within one (1) year after the cause of action has accrued, or they shall be deemed waived and barred.
